steps.title: Define the task requirement
steps.content: The student wants to place Einstein's argument within its historical context. That requires three things: (1) identifying Einstein's specific argument, (2) locating it in the timeline of quantum theory's development, and (3) showing how it fits the broader story of the field.

steps.title: Eliminate and match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘During the dawn of quantum theory, Einstein maintained the independent reality of some subatomic entities, although Bohr's opposing interpretation would become the widely accepted view.’: Names Einstein's argument, places it at the birth of quantum theory, and shows its historical outcome. All three requirements met. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘At the 1927 Solvay Conference on Physics, Einstein disagreed with Bohr's argument that subatomic entities like electrons had a reality independent of observation.’: Attributes the "independent reality" argument to Bohr, not Einstein — factually backwards per the notes. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The attendees of the 1927 Solvay Conference were among the preeminent scientists of their era, including Einstein, who opposed Bohr's proposal.’: Never states Einstein's actual argument, only that he opposed Bohr. No historical context for quantum theory's development. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In 1927, Einstein and Bohr engaged in a famous debate; Bohr's argument, later called the Copenhagen interpretation, would remain popular decades after.’: Mentions the debate and Bohr's staying power, but never identifies Einstein's position. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
